Fastify, esbuild, and Nx","width":1400,"height":735}]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"mx-auto max-w-7xl px-4 lg:px-8","data-content-area":true,"children":["$","div",null,{"className":"relative xl:block","children":"$undefined"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"w-full min-w-0 md:mx-auto md:max-w-screen-md","children":["$undefined",["$","div",null,{"data-document":"main","className":"prose prose-lg prose-slate dark:prose-invert w-full max-w-none","children":["$","article",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"children":"There are many decisions to make when it comes to building a Node API. There are a variety of frameworks to choose from (Express, Fastify, Koa, etc.), and a million different ways to build and deploy the application."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"In this article, I'll show you the easiest way to go from zero to production by using Nx to create a Node API project."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["We'll be using ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://www.fastify.io/","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Fastify","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," as the framework of choice. Fastify is a fast (as the name implies) and low-overhead server in Node. It has grown in popularity, recently crossing the 1 million weekly download mark on npm. I'm a fan of Fastify's plugin architecture, and the ecosystem is quite impressive, boasting over ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://www.fastify.io/ecosystem/","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"250 core and community plugins","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Table of We've got you covered!"}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"text-center","children":[" ",["$","iframe",null,{"src":"https://www.youtube.com/embed/K4f-fMuAoRY","title":"$undefined","width":"100%","allow":"accelerometer; autoplay; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; fullscreen","loading":"lazy","credentialless":"true","className":"rounded-lg shadow-lg"}],"$undefined"]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"creating-the-project","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Creating the project",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#creating-the-project","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"You can create a new API with a single command."}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"$$ npx create-nx-workspace@latest \\\n--preset=node-standalone \\ # create a Node.js project\n--framework=fastify \\      # other options are express and koa\n--docker                   # we'll touch on this later on\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["To run the server in dev-mode, use ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npx nx serve"}]," (aliased to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npm start"}],"), and you should see the server starting at port 3000."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"$$ curl <http://localhost:3000>\n{\"message\":\"Hello API\"}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"A couple of notable files:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":["The ",["$","code",null,{"children":"src/main.ts"}]," file is responsible for starting the Fastify server and registering plugins."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":["The ",["$","code",null,{"children":"src/app/app.ts"}]," file is the app plugin that provides an initial endpoint at ",["$","code",null,{"children":"/"}]," that replies with ",["$","code",null,{"children":"{\"message\": \"Hello API\"}"}],"."]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["When you edit the source code, the server will reload. You can pass ",["$","code",null,{"children":"--no-watch"}]," to disable this behavior."]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"running-tests","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Running tests",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#running-tests","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"In additional to generating the source code, Nx will also create two test suites:"}],["$","ol",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":["Unit tests via ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npx nx test"}]," (aliased to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npm run test"}],")."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":["E2E tests via ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npx nx e2e e2e"}]," (aliased to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npm run e2e"}],")."]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Unit tests take advantage of Fastify's plugin architecture, and allows you to test each plugin in isolation. It runs using ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://jestjs.io/","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Jest","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", which is the most popular test runner in Node."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"// src/app/app.spec.ts\n// This file is generated by Nx.\nimport Fastify, { FastifyInstance } from 'fastify';\nimport { app } from './app';\n\ndescribe('GET /', () => {\n let server: FastifyInstance; beforeEach(() => {\n server = Fastify();\n server.register(app);\n }); it('should respond with a message', async () => {\n const response = await server.inject({\n method: 'GET',\n url: '/',\n }); expect(response.json()).toEqual({ message: 'Hello API' });\n });\n});\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"The E2E tests run against the actual server (with all plugins registered), which gives better real-world guarantees."}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"$$ npx nx serve & # run server in background\n$ npx nx e2e e2e # run test suite\nGET /\n    ✓ should return a message (27 ms)\n\nTest Suites: 1 passed, 1 total\nTests: 1 passed, 1 total\nSnapshots: 0 total\nTime: 0.429 s\nRan all test suites.Tearing down... ——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————— >\n\nNX Successfully ran target e2e for project e2e (3s)$ lsof -i:3000 -t | xargs kill # stop server process\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"There are trade-offs between speed versus confidence when it comes to unit versus E2E tests, which is a topic that is out of scope for this article. I think you should do both, but this is a discussion be held with your team. Nx supports both cases out of the box."}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"building-for-production-using-esbuild","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Building for production using esbuild",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#building-for-production-using-esbuild","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Now that we have our production-ready app, let's examine how Nx handles the build process using ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://esbuild.github.io/","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":["$","code",null,{"children":"esbuild"}],"target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","code",null,{"children":"esbuild"}]," is a bundler written in Go that is extremely fast — it is much faster than other bundlers like webpack and parcel, but that may change in the future as other tools make their own speed improvements."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["When you run the build command, Nx uses ",["$","code",null,{"children":"esbuild"}]," to generate a self-contained app bundle, which does not require ",["$","code",null,{"children":"node_modules"}],"."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"$$ npx nx build # aliased to npm build\n> nx run api:build\n\n\n—————————————————————————————————————————————————————\n\nNX Successfully ran target build for project api (2s)\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["A cool feature of Nx, is that commands such as ",["$","code",null,{"children":"build"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"test"}]," are cached if the project (or its dependencies) have not changed. If we run the build a second time, you'll see it completes in a few milliseconds."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"$$ npx nx build\n> nx run api:build  # existing outputs match the cache, left as is\n\n\n——————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————————— >\n\nNX Successfully ran target build for project api (16ms) Nx read the output from the cache instead of running the command for 1 out of 1 tasks.\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"We'll touch more on the caching when we look at Docker support."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"And now that the server bundle is ready, we can run it."}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"\n$ node dist/api\n{\"level\":30,\"time\":1675880059720,\"pid\":70605,\"hostname\":\"mbp.lan\",\"msg\":\"Server listening at http://\\[::1\\]:3000\"}\n{\"level\":30,\"time\":1675880059721,\"pid\":70605,\"hostname\":\"mbp.lan\",\"msg\":\"Server listening at <http://127.0.0.1:3000>\"}\n[ ready ] <http://localhost:3000>\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["You can even run the e2e suite against the production bundle: ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npx nx e2e e2e"}],"."]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"docker-support","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Docker support",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#docker-support","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Recall that we passed the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"--docker"}]," option when creating the API project. WIth this option, Nx will generate a default ",["$","code",null,{"children":"Dockerfile"}]," and a ",["$","code",null,{"children":"docker-build"}]," target."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"# This file is generated by Nx.\n#\n# Build the docker image with `npx nx docker-build api`.\n# Tip: Modify \"docker-build\" options in project.json to change docker build args.\n#\n# Run the container with `docker run -p 3000:3000 -t api`.\nFROM docker.io/node:lts-alpine\n\nENV HOST=0.0.0.0\nENV PORT=3000WORKDIR /appRUN addgroup --system api && \\\n adduser --system -G api apiCOPY dist/api api\nRUN chown -R api:api .CMD [ \"node\", \"api\" ]\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["To build the image, run ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npx nx docker-build"}],". The image copies only the self-contained bundle, so no ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npm install"}]," at all!"]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Nx is smart enough to bundle the app before building the Docker image — because of the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"dependsOn"}]," configuration in ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project.json"}],". You can visualize this dependency with ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npx nx graph"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2023-02-28/HRfoF6hPy-XH-MSL42PSIQ.avif","alt":"","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Now that the image is built, we can run it."}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"\n$ docker run -p 3000:3000 -t api\n{\"level\":30,\"time\":1675880993256,\"pid\":1,\"hostname\":\"248744de020a\",\"msg\":\"Server listening at <http://0.0.0.0:3000>\"}\n[ ready ] <http://0.0.0.0:3000>\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Note:"}]," The server binds to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"0.0.0.0"}]," so that it can be access from the host machine. You can run curl to verify that it indeed works, or better yet use the E2E test suite (",["$","code",null,{"children":"npx nx e2e e2e"}],")!"]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"deploying-the-server","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Deploying the server",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#deploying-the-server","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["There are numerous platforms that we can deploy our app to. I like ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://fly.io","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Fly.io","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," since it very easy to deploy all over the world using the CLI, and it comes with good ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://fly.io/docs/languages-and-frameworks/dockerfile/","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Docker support","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["If you haven't used Fly before, please follow their ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://fly.io/docs/speedrun/","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"short getting started guide","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," (5–10 mins)."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Once you are ready, let's configure our project."}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"\n$ fly launch --generate-name --no-deploy\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Follow the prompts and a ",["$","code",null,{"children":"fly.toml"}]," file will be generated, which contains the Fly configuration. We need to update this file with the correct port used by our image."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"\n[[services]]\n http_checks = []\n internal_port = 3000 # Make sure this matches what the app listens on\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Now we can deploy the app."}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"\n$ fly deploy\n\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Fly will log out the monitoring link when the app is successfully deployed."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2023-02-28/oAuTGrLZCV87OEC0e-6KpQ.avif","alt":"","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["And you can open the deployed server using ",["$","code",null,{"children":"fly open"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2023-02-28/tV8aAtjRoRMwlj1Tv17IAw.avif","alt":"","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"That's it!
